next_step_text: Small update as I am in Struer today and have talked to finance. As finance are swamped in the year end activities June/May, we have pushed next step to later in June.

recent_activities_14d.subject: Jonas Elber and finance to deliver business case for internal approval
Finance (Bang & Olufsen) to deliver business case for internal approval

our_open_deliverables.text: Deliver commercial proposals for CLM and P2P modules with separate pricing to Jonas Elber.
Share demo videos for additional modules (contract management, long-tail spend).
Provide ballpark price estimates for integration, licensing, and implementation to Nickolaj.
Send three customer reference videos to Nickolaj showing CLM and P2P implementations.
Record and send text-based session covering the tax engine demo by mid next month.

buyer_dependent_items.text: Provide user breakdown and answers to Asmita's commercial-proposal questions.
Schedule and confirm next steps post-finance year-end workload (gated, pushed to late June 2026).
Champion to finish the business case from the new proposal and bring it to the CFO/IT-and-digital board.

competitive_position.summary: Zycus is a finalist in a down-selected field but the rivals are unnamed. As of 13 Apr 2026, Zycus is shortlisted among 3 vendors for P2P and CLM vendor was already selected (23 Jan 2026) - the strongest current dynamic. The incumbent being displaced is the custom 'Bear Shop' app on SAP ECC (slated for decommission), with the broader stack described as 'SAP and Oracle for P2P' and a likely future move to Microsoft Dynamics. The single strongest current threat is whichever of the two unnamed co-finalists is still live in the P2P bake-off; Zycus's edge is AI/Merlin depth (Jonas rated it ahead of competition, 25 Nov 2025) and dual-ERP support.

competitors.name: Unknown SharePoint-layer CLM vendor
competitors.quote: I can be very transparent with you, we are looking into a solution that sits on top of SharePoint today.
competitors.how_we_win: Position Zycus CLM as a purpose-built AI-native platform (contract risk monitor, Merlin negotiation agent, InstaReview) vs. a SharePoint overlay, and anchor to Jonas's stated pain: 10,000 contracts in SharePoint are already difficult to manage, so a layer on top of the same broken foundation will not solve the problem. Prove the AI differentiation with a live contract-discovery POC on B&O's SharePoint repository.

competitors.name: SAP ECC Bear Shop (incumbent, being decommissioned)
competitors.quote: SAP ECC is a really old platform, and we have developed an application on top of this which we call Bear Shop... it has a lot of errors, a lot of limitations, and it cannot support dual ERPs. This application will be decommissioned during next year.
competitors.how_we_win: Incumbent is already defeated (being decommissioned). No active threat.

competitors.name: Unknown SharePoint-native CLM vendor
competitors.quote: We are looking into a solution that sits on top of SharePoint today (Jonas Elber, 21 Nov 2025 CLM demo)
competitors.source: Avoma CLM demo 21 Nov 2025, Jonas Elber
competitors.how_we_win: Zycus AI advantage (InstaReview, Negogenie, Merlin metadata extraction, contract risk monitor) + Word integration (WordConnect plugin, validated by Christine 21 Nov) + proven bulk SharePoint migration (AI-powered, demoed 21 Nov). Position Zycus as the AI-first CLM that enhances SharePoint rather than replacing it, with faster time-to-value than a native SharePoint layer. Surface the competitor name and feature set, then run a head-to-head AI capability demo.

competitors.name: Microsoft Dynamics P2P (potential)
competitors.quote: We are a small company to run two large ERPs. I would be surprised if we don't go the Microsoft way, but there needs to be a decision (Jonas Elber, 20 Nov 2025)
competitors.source: Avoma alignment call 20 Nov 2025, Jonas Elber
competitors.how_we_win: Dual-ERP support (SAP ECC + Dynamics) is Zycus's differentiator, demoed 21 and 27 Nov 2025. Position Zycus as ERP-agnostic, allowing B&O to run both ERPs in parallel during transition without P2P lock-in. If B&O moves to Dynamics, Zycus integrates; if they stay SAP, Zycus integrates. Microsoft Dynamics P2P locks B&O into Microsoft's roadmap and may not support SAP ECC well. Make the Merlin AI invoice processing (AP Smartdesk, 3-way matching, auto-extraction) the Phase 2 centerpiece, as Microsoft's AI for P2P is less mature.

competitors.name: Bear Shop (SAP ECC custom app, incumbent P2P)
competitors.quote: Bear Shop has a lot of errors and limitations, and it cannot support dual ERP solutions. This application will be decommissioned during next year (Jonas Elber, 20 Nov 2025)
competitors.source: Avoma alignment call 20 Nov 2025, Jonas Elber
competitors.how_we_win: Bear Shop is explicitly being decommissioned and is not a competitive threat. It is the pain Zycus is solving. Use Bear Shop's limitations (errors, no dual-ERP, free-text reliance, poor spend visibility) as the baseline pain in every commercial and board presentation.

competitors.name: SharePoint (incumbent CLM repository)
competitors.quote: 10,000 contracts stored in SharePoint, difficult to manage (Jonas Elber, 21 Nov 2025 CLM demo)
competitors.source: Avoma CLM demo 21 Nov 2025, Jonas Elber
competitors.how_we_win: SharePoint is the pain Zycus is solving for CLM. It has no lifecycle management, no AI, no approval workflows, no risk monitoring. Zycus's bulk SharePoint migration with AI metadata extraction (demoed 21 Nov) allows B&O to keep SharePoint as the storage layer while Zycus adds the intelligence and process layer on top. This is a 'both/and' positioning, not a rip-and-replace.

competitors.name: Two unnamed shortlisted vendors (P2P final field)
competitors.quote: P2P approved. Zycus Shortlisted between 3 vendors. Anthony to align with Jonas around pricing.
competitors.how_we_win: Identify the two rivals via Jonas, then anchor on AI/Merlin invoice intake and dual-ERP handling where they are weakest.

competitors.name: SAP ECC 'Bear Shop' (incumbent custom P2P app)
competitors.quote: It is an application that has a lot of errors and limitations, and it cannot support dual ERP solutions. So this application will be decommissioned during next year.
competitors.how_we_win: Position Zycus as the modern dual-ERP replacement that removes free-text spend; incumbent is already being retired.

competitors.name: Microsoft Dynamics (future ERP direction)
competitors.quote: I would be surprised if we don't go Microsoft way, but of course there needs to be a decision on this.
competitors.how_we_win: Emphasize Zycus handles multiple ERPs natively, so the Dynamics path does not require re-platforming procurement.

champion_strength.source: SF Next_Step_History 29 May 2026 + Avoma CLM demo part 2, 25 Nov 2025
champion_strength.summary: Jonas Elber is a strong, recently strengthened champion: he is the decision-taker on CLM, drove every demo, rated Zycus's AI ahead of competition, and was promoted to Head of Technology while preparing the business case from the new proposal. The risk is that his advocacy has not yet pulled in the CFO/finance economic buyer and the deal remains single-threaded.

sources: Avoma CLM demo part 2, 25 Nov 2025 (Jonas Elber)
SF Next_Step 16 Jun 2026 (finance conversation in Struer)
meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer is named by role but not engaged. On 25 Nov 2025 Jonas was explicit about the approval chain: 'the recommendation that the team does need to go to an IT and digital board where our CFO is involved... we have different stakeholders making the final decision of each of these projects.' He also confirmed on the P2P side 'the system owner is finance' while he himself owns the CLM decision. So budget authority sits with the CFO-chaired IT/digital board and a finance system owner, neither of whom has appeared on any call. Salesforce X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c and Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c are all blank/false. The 23 Jun activity shows Casper met finance on-site in Struer, which is progress, but no finance/CFO decision-maker is mapped. Securing a direct line to the finance system owner and the board sponsor is the gating move for pricing sign-off.

recommended_moves: Ask Jonas to introduce the finance P2P system owner now that year-end close is ending.
Co-build a quantified business case with Jonas on free-text spend leakage and AP effort.
Ask Jonas which two vendors remain in the P2P shortlist and tailor AI/dual-ERP positioning.
